signals.title: USD/JPY — Hawkish Fed + Intervention Risk + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.59, breaking above the 160.00 intervention watch level following the hawkish Fed dot plot and Chair Warsh's comments. The Fed held rates but signaled hikes ahead, lifting dollar yields. The 296bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.69% vs TONA 0.73%) continues to sustain the bid, despite extreme JPY COT LF net short −115,036. Options market shows moderate put skew (25d RR -1.09), indicating hedging demand for JPY strength, but this is currently overwhelmed by carry and USD strength. Intervention risk is elevated on a sustained break above 160.00. Watch for any BoJ verbal warnings or direct action.

signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + COT vs OIS Divergence + Hawkish Fed
signals.text: NZD is the weakest G10 currency this week (NZD 1W avg: -0.715% vs USD 1W avg: +0.677%, G10 spread: 1.391%), exacerbated by the hawkish Fed. NZD/USD COT LF net short −22,354 (long%=23.9%) indicates crowded speculative short positioning, while the RBNZ has a market bias for a hike. This COT vs OIS divergence creates significant short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ delivers on its hawkish bias or if Q1 GDP (due Thursday) surprises to the upside. The current -83bp real rate (RBNZ 2.25% vs infl_exp 3.08%) suggests an accommodative stance, making a hike more impactful. Watch for NZD Q1 GDP data tomorrow.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Hawkish Fed + Soft UK CPI + Elevated Vol Premium
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3290 is under pressure from both a hawkish Fed and softer-than-expected UK CPI data. The Fed's hawkish tilt supports USD strength, while the BoE remains on hold at 3.75% (0bp carry vs Fed). Options market shows elevated vol premium (GBP/USD IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.5%, VRP +4.5%) and a mild put skew (25d RR -0.67), indicating strong hedging demand for GBP downside protection. This suggests traders are pricing in further weakness for GBP/USD. The next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) tomorrow (18 Jun) is a key watch level for any shift in stance.

signals.title: EUR/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Hawkish Fed + Elevated Vol
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1506 faces headwinds from the hawkish Fed, with the 176bp USD carry premium (SOFR 3.69% vs ESTR 1.93%) favoring the dollar. Speculative positioning shows EUR COT LF net short −49,290, while the ECB has a market bias for a hike. This COT vs OIS divergence creates short-squeeze risk if the ECB delivers on its hawkish bias at the next meeting (23 Jul). Options market shows elevated vol premium (EUR/USD IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.7%, VRP +2.4%) and a mild put skew (25d RR -0.36), indicating hedging demand for EUR downside. Watch for ECB commentary ahead of their next meeting.
